description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:53: SecretValue exposes its bytes only via .as_bytes() -> &[u8]. There is no impl AsRef<[u8]> for SecretValue.

Idiomatic Rust: anything that wraps a byte slice for which 'pass me to a function that takes AsRef<[u8]>' is a sensible thing to do should impl AsRef<[u8]>. Examples in std: String, Vec<u8>, OsString, PathBuf — all impl AsRef of their underlying slice/str/Path. Today, callers of e.g. std::fs::write(path, secret.as_bytes()) work fine — but a function with signature 'fn(impl AsRef<[u8]>)' (signing APIs, hashers) requires the explicit .as_bytes() call.

Risk consideration: AsRef<[u8]> would make it slightly easier to accidentally pass a SecretValue into a logging API that takes 'impl AsRef<[u8]>' and writes the bytes to a buffer. But the same hazard exists today through .as_bytes(); the impl wouldn't change the threat model materially.

Recommendation: add 'impl AsRef<[u8]> for SecretValue { fn as_ref(&self) -> &[u8] { &self.0 } }'. Keep .as_bytes() as the documented primary accessor (more discoverable in rustdoc); the AsRef impl is for generic compatibility.
title: SecretValue does not implement AsRef<[u8]>
